behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: This is a pure key-derivation correctness property at a single function/object boundary (determinism + collision resistance for input sets). No OS/network/subprocess, no external side-effect round-trip, and no AI dependency are stated. Higher levels were considered and rejected because the behavior text only specifies key generation logic, not cross-module handoff or infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Property-style unit test on the key builder: same normalized parameter map always yields the same key; varied parameter maps (order, casing rules, value differences) do not produce equal keys within the tested domain.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: Failure can appear at module boundaries: category -> config lookup -> TTL calculator. Unit tests of TTL math alone can miss miswiring/misloaded category mapping. System was considered and rejected because behavior text does not require real external contract checks or external side-effect readback; this can be validated in-process with real modules wired together.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test loading real freshness config and TTL service together: request TTL for categories like `user_profiles` and `product_catalog`, assert returned durations match configured values and fallback/default behavior for unknown category.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff across local modules (memory metric provider, config, strategy selector). Unit tests for selector logic can miss wiring errors. System was considered and rejected because the behavior text does not explicitly require real OS memory instrumentation or external response/round-trip contracts; injected memory metrics in an in-process integration test provide the needed signal.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with real strategy-selection pipeline: start below threshold and assert LRU active; drive reported usage above threshold and assert LFU becomes active according to config, including boundary value behavior at the threshold.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires an external side effect across a real boundary: PostgreSQL write -> cache invalidation visibility. This matches SYSTEM TRIAD trigger #3 (prove externally visible side effect via round-trip readback). Integration with fakes can hide real failures in DB-trigger/notification/transaction semantics. Higher levels (Agentic/Workflow) are disqualified because no AI/ML decision-making is involved.
behaviors.test_description: System test against a real PostgreSQL instance and running cache layer: seed DB + warm matching cache keys, perform real table write/update, then round-trip read affected keys and verify they are invalidated/missed while unaffected keys remain valid.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Although policy algorithms can be unit-tested, this behavior can fail when cache access tracking, capacity enforcement, and selected policy interact. An integration check across those modules avoids false confidence from unit-only tests. System was considered and rejected because no explicit real external contract or external round-trip side effect is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with real cache store + policy wiring: fill cache to capacity, perform controlled access patterns, trigger one additional insert, and assert the evicted key matches LRU expectations; repeat under LFU mode and assert least-frequently-used eviction.
